question: FULL AUDIT of Alpha-Omega before PyPI publish. The tool has grown from a single script to a full package in one session. Review ALL code for: (1) Security: git command injection, path traversal, subprocess safety (2) Reliability: error handling, timeout handling, graceful degradation (3) Code quality: dead code, inconsistencies, missing edge cases (4) API design: CLI UX, command naming, flag consistency (5) Packaging: pyproject.toml completeness, entry points, version (6) Known issues from prior review: review.py not visible in branch diff, zero test coverage, silent error masking in _run_git, mixed language tokenization. Be specific: file:line, what's wrong, how to fix.

implementation_brief.goal: FULL AUDIT of Alpha-Omega before PyPI publish. The tool has grown from a single script to a full package in one session. Review ALL code for: (1) Security: git command injection, path traversal, subprocess safety (2) Reliability: error handling, timeout handling, graceful degradation (3) Code quality: dead code, inconsistencies, missing edge cases (4) API design: CLI UX, command naming, flag consistency (5) Packaging: pyproject.toml completeness, entry points, version (6) Known issues from prior review: review.py not visible in branch diff, zero test coverage, silent error masking in _run_git, mixed language tokenization. Be specific: file:line, what's wrong, how to fix.
implementation_brief.winning_thesis: Block PyPI until a short prepublish hardening pass fixes the concrete defects already visible in this repo: path containment, review coverage, lock cleanup, backward-compatible memory indexing, test discoverability, and packaging/docs drift. This is the smallest option that turns the tool from interesting prototype into defensible public alpha.
constraints: Delays release slightly, likely 1-2 focused days.
Strict `session_id` validation may reject any ad hoc old files not named like `ao_<digits>`.
This still leaves deeper design cleanup for later, such as duplicated `setup`/`init` logic and least-privilege execution modes.
